conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have a good understanding of the eligibility rules. To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 18 years old. I'd like to find out if I can still claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see that Alex is 18 years old, which means he's over 16. 

For children aged 16-19, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if they're in approved education or training. 

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (This typically includes things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or similar qualifications at school or college.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! Alex is doing his A-levels at sixth form college at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! A-levels at sixth form college count as approved education.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex:

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (currently around £25 per week for the eldest child) towards his upkeep - for example, on food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is Alex in paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or is he claiming any benefits in his own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working that much - he just focuses on his studies. And he's not claiming any benefits himself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Even though Alex is 18, you can continue to claim Child Benefit because he's under 20 and is in approved education (his A-levels at sixth form college). Since you live in the UK, Alex lives with you, nobody else is claiming for him, and he's not working 24+ hours per week or claiming his own benefits, all the eligibility criteria are met.

**Important to note:** You'll continue to be eligible as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old. If his circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education, starts working 24+ hours per week, or starts claiming certain benefits), you should contact the Child Benefit Office to let them know.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is currently doing A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education. Under the Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK (Bristol), lives with Alex, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week nor claiming benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies under the extended eligibility rules because he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The claimant lives in the UK, lives with Alex, is responsible for him, and nobody else is claiming for him. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits in his own right.

Under the Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK (Bristol), lives with Alex, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week nor claiming benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies under the extended eligibility rules because he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The claimant lives in the UK, lives with Alex, is responsible for him, and nobody else is claiming for him. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits in his own right.
